description:
You are a platform engineer who needs to keep an eye on how much space
    various CI/CD work-folders are occupying.

    Premises already present on the system
    --------------------------------------
    1. Directory tree to analyse:  /home/user/ci_cd_workspace
       ├── build_artifacts   (contains 10 binary files of exactly 100 KiB each)
       ├── cache             (contains 3 compressed files of exactly 1 MiB each)
       ├── logs              (contains 15 text files of exactly 20 KiB each)
       ├── temp              (contains 5 temporary files of exactly 50 KiB each)
       └── scripts           (contains 2 helper scripts of exactly 5 KiB each)

    2. Your task is *only* to create a summarised, machine-parseable usage
       report; do **not** delete or modify any of the above data.

    What you must achieve
    ---------------------
    A. Measure the apparent size (on-disk space actually occupied, not the
       theoretical byte-count) of each **direct** sub-directory inside
       /home/user/ci_cd_workspace.

    B. Produce a plain-text log file called
       /home/user/ci_disk_usage.log
       that lists exactly the five (5) sub-directories mentioned above,
       **sorted in descending order by size**.

    C. The log file must have precisely one directory per line and *nothing
       else*:  
           <absolute_path_to_directory>,<size_in_KiB_as_decimal_integer>

       • Size unit must be Kibibytes (1 KiB = 1024 bytes).  
       • Use no thousands separators, no additional spaces.  
       • Example of the required formatting style (do *not* hard-code
         these numbers; the test will verify the real values):

         /some/path,1234

    D. Do not print anything extraneous to the log file.  Console output is
       fine but it will not be graded—only the contents of
       /home/user/ci_disk_usage.log will be checked.

    Verification details the grader will look for
    ---------------------------------------------
    • File /home/user/ci_disk_usage.log **exists**.  
    • The file contains exactly five lines.  
    • Each line follows the format  
          /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/<subdir>,<integer>  
      with the integer matching the actual “du -sk” value for that specific
      sub-directory.  
    • Lines are in strictly descending order by the integer value.  
    • No extra blank lines, headers, or trailing spaces are present.

    Typical command-line steps (for your reference)
    -----------------------------------------------
    You will need only standard GNU/Linux tools already available in every
    container (e.g., du, sort, head, printf, etc.).  The entire job can be
    achieved in three or four separate commands or one compound pipeline;
    the exact approach is up to you.

    Remember: create **/home/user/ci_disk_usage.log** with the exact format
    described above.
truth:
Initial directory layout and file sizes (pre-created before the task):
    /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/
        build_artifacts/
            artifact_01.bin … artifact_10.bin  – 10 × 102400 bytes  = 1000 KiB total
        cache/
            layer_01.tgz, layer_02.tgz, layer_03.tgz  – 3 × 1048576 bytes = 3072 KiB total
        logs/
            log_01.txt … log_15.txt             – 15 × 20480 bytes  = 300 KiB total
        temp/
            tmp_01.dat … tmp_05.dat             – 5 × 51200 bytes   = 250 KiB total
        scripts/
            build.sh, deploy.sh                 – 2 × 5120 bytes    = 10 KiB total

    Expected content of /home/user/ci_disk_usage.log after successful task completion:
        /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/cache,3072
        /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/build_artifacts,1000
        /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/logs,300
        /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/temp,250
        /home/user/ci_cd_workspace/scripts,10
